_provisional_note: PROVISIONAL misconception bank for the linear_motion WU-AAS subject, authored ahead of the real mint (see the sibling problems/ file for the full rationale). Reconcile against the real bank once F2 mints this subject.

misconceptions.display_name: Forgets the 1/2 factor in the position equation
misconceptions.description: Computes distance as x = v0*t + a*t^2 instead of x = v0*t + (1/2)*a*t^2, doubling the displacement from constant acceleration.
trigger_phrases: distance is v0 times t plus a times t squared
no need for the one half
just multiply acceleration by time squared and add it

misconceptions.description: Treats the acceleration as changing over the interval (e.g. averaging or re-deriving it at each instant) instead of using the given constant rate directly in the kinematics equations.
trigger_phrases: acceleration probably changes over the five seconds
we can't assume it stays at 2.0
the rate is only constant for part of the trip
